title: Acilepis gardneri
taxonomy: Acilepis gardneri is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is native to Western Australia and is a member of the tribe Gnaphalieae. The plant is classified as a shrub and is known for its small, yellow daisy-like flowers. Acilepis gardneri is a relatively rare species and is often found in coastal heathlands and woodlands. Its classification is further defined by its genus and species designation, Acilepis gardneri.
uses: Acilepis gardneri is native to Western Australia and has been traditionally used by the Indigenous people for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and skin conditions. In addition, it has been used to make a poultice to treat sore eyes and to relieve pain and inflammation. intro: Acilepis gardneri, also known as Australian chamomile or blue chamomile, is a perennial herb native to Australia, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, which may help in soothing anxiety and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Acilepis gardneri include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
